commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
So. The Lord permits evil, but he does not approve of it. (Calmet)
Homer makes Jupiter send a deluding dream to Agamemnon. (Iliad B.)
The delusive predictions of impostors, are one great source of calamities. (Du Hamel)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 2 Chronicles 18:21 And he said, I will go out, and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And the LORD said, You shall entice him, and you shall also prevail: go out, and do even so. In the case of Ahab, God said, “Who shall deceive Ahab for me. ” In addition, there is also the fact that he always made himself available for comparison with the pagan gods, and all this is below the dignity of God. However, viewed in another way, it becomes worthy of him. I say this for he is so merciful that for the sake of our salvation he foregoes being spoken of in terms befitting his dignity.
